name: Sint Maarten

colors.symbolism: the upper field; courage and unity
peace and the white sand beaches; the hoist triangle bearing the arms
the lower field; the surrounding Caribbean Sea and the open sky
details in the coat of arms — the rising sun and the courthouse
details in the coat of arms — the bouquet of yellow sage and the foliage
details in the coat of arms — the orange-yellow rising sun

description: A field divided per pall (three regions meeting at a point on the horizontal meridian): a red triangle above, a blue triangle below, and a white isoceles triangle based on the hoist. The coat of arms is centred on the white hoist triangle.
history: Adopted on 13 June 1985 when Sint Maarten was an island territory within the Netherlands Antilles. It was retained as the national flag when Sint Maarten became a separate constituent country of the Kingdom of the Netherlands on 10 October 2010, following the dissolution of the Netherlands Antilles.

features.symbolism: the field is divided per pall into three triangles — red above for courage and unity, blue below for the Caribbean Sea and open sky, and a white hoist triangle for peace and the white sand beaches

features.role: national coat of arms within the white hoist triangle
features.symbolism: the coat of arms is centred on the white hoist triangle and depicts the courthouse of Philipsburg, a bouquet of yellow sage (the national flower) and the monument commemorating the Treaty of Concordia (1648); above is a rising sun, and a banner below carries the motto 'Semper Pro Grediens' ('Always Progressing'). The brown pelican appears as a crest
